headline: GPT-5.5 pricing surge forces healthcare AI budget recalculation
body: OpenRouter reports significant price increases for GPT-5.5, creating immediate cost implications for healthcare organizations deploying large language models at scale. Clinical teams using GPT-based tools for documentation, clinical decision support, and workflow automation must reassess total cost of ownership and vendor contracts. The pricing shift coincides with emerging competition from local LLMs and specialized reasoning models, giving healthcare IT leaders options to evaluate based on performance-per-dollar rather than brand alone. For consulting firms advising health systems on AI implementation, this signals the need to pressure-test multiple model vendors and openly challenge assumptions about enterprise LLM strategy.
